description: Slack bot that handles marketing intake via conversation or form, generates briefs, creates Google Drive folders, sets up Monday.com tasks, and notifies the marketing team

userStories.title: Initialize Node.js project with TypeScript and Bolt.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a project scaffold with Slack Bolt.js SDK, TypeScript, and basic configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json with dependencies: @slack/bolt, @anthropic-ai/sdk, typescript, tsx, dotenv
tsconfig.json configured for Node.js with strict mode
src/index.ts with Bolt app initialization (socket mode)
.env.example listing required env vars: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_APP_TOKEN, SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
.gitignore excludes node_modules, .env, dist
npm run dev script starts the bot with tsx watch
npm run build compiles TypeScript
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation — everything else depends on this. Use Bolt.js socket mode for local dev.

userStories.title: Add SQLite database with conversations and projects tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database to store conversation state and project records.
acceptanceCriteria: Install better-sqlite3 dependency
src/lib/db.ts creates SQLite database at data/marcomsbot.db

conversations table with columns: id, user_id, user_name, channel_id, thread_ts, status (gathering|confirming|complete|cancelled), current_step, collected_data (JSON text), classification (quick|full|undetermined), created_at, updated_at, expires_at

projects table with columns: id, name, type (quick|full), requester_name, requester_slack_id, requester_email, division, status, drive_folder_url, brief_doc_url, monday_item_id, monday_url, source (conversation|form), created_at, due_date
divisions table with columns: id, name, slack_channel (nullable)
Database initializes tables on first run
Export helper functions: getConversation, upsertConversation, createProject, getProject, searchProjects
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same SQLite pattern as pearl-command-center. No migrations — create tables if not exists.

userStories.title: Handle Slack @mention events and route by intent
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want to @mention MarcomsBot and have it understand what I need.
acceptanceCriteria: Bot listens for app_mention events via Bolt.js
Bot also listens for direct messages (message.im)
When mentioned with no clear intent, bot sends greeting with chat vs form options (numbered list)
When mentioned with 'help', bot sends help menu listing capabilities
When mentioned with status keywords ('status of', 'where are we on', 'update on'), route to status handler (stub for now)
When mentioned with search keywords ('find the brief', 'link to'), route to search handler (stub for now)
Otherwise, route to intake conversation handler
All bot replies are threaded (reply in thread, not channel)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec: bot must always reply in-thread. Greeting must offer chat vs form. Intent detection can be simple keyword matching for now.

userStories.title: Implement conversation state machine for intake flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a state machine to track where each user is in the intake conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/conversation.ts exports ConversationManager class
Tracks state per user_id: gathering, confirming, complete, cancelled
collected_data tracks: division, request_type, description, deliverables[], due_date, due_date_parsed, urgency, details, assets_mentioned[], additional_info
getNextQuestion() returns the next unanswered required field
markFieldCollected(field, value) updates collected_data
isComplete() returns true when all required fields are populated
toSummary() returns formatted Slack mrkdwn summary matching UX spec confirmation template
State persists to SQLite via db helpers
Conversations expire after 24 hours of inactivity (expires_at field)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core state machine. Does not include Claude interpretation yet — that comes next.

userStories.title: Add Claude API for natural language interpretation
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want to describe my request naturally and have the bot understand me.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/claude.ts exports interpretMessage(message, conversationState) function
Uses Anthropic SDK to call Claude API with system prompt explaining MarcomsBot's role
Claude extracts structured data from free-text: division, request_type, description, deliverables, due_date, urgency, assets
Returns JSON with extracted fields and confidence
Handles bundled responses (user provides multiple fields at once) — extracts all available info
Handles natural date parsing ('Friday' → next Friday, 'end of month' → date, 'ASAP' → urgent flag)
Handles natural division detection ('I'm on the people team' → HR)
Export classifyRequest(collectedData) that returns 'quick' | 'full' | 'undetermined' based on deliverable count, keywords, complexity
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec Pass 4: bot must skip questions when info already provided. Claude handles the intelligent extraction.

userStories.title: Wire up conversational intake flow end-to-end
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want to chat with @MarcomsBot, answer questions conversationally, and have my request captured.
acceptanceCriteria: When intake conversation starts, bot asks first unanswered question with examples
Each user reply is sent to Claude for interpretation
Extracted fields are saved to conversation state
Bot skips questions for fields already collected (says 'Got most of what I need!' when applicable)
When all required fields collected, bot sends confirmation summary matching UX spec format
User can reply 'yes' to confirm
User can describe changes ('actually make it due Thursday') — bot updates and re-confirms
User can say 'start over' to reset conversation
User can say 'cancel' to cancel
Bot never auto-submits — always requires explicit 'yes'
Bot identifies itself as intake assistant, not a creative worker (UX spec constraint #9)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This wires together US-003, US-004, US-005 into the full chat flow. Critical UX constraint: never auto-submit.

userStories.title: Add brief generation via Claude API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Claude to generate mini-briefs and full creative briefs from collected intake data.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/brief-generator.ts exports generateBrief(collectedData, classification) function
For 'quick' classification: generates mini-brief with fields: requester, division, date, request summary, deliverable, key details, due date, assets/references

For 'full' classification: generates full creative brief with fields: requester, division, dates, project overview, objectives, target audience, deliverables list, key messages, specifications, timeline table, brand/style notes, assets provided, references, additional notes
Returns brief as markdown string
Brief content is generated by Claude based on collected_data — not just template fill-in
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Templates from PRD. Claude generates the narrative sections, not just slot-filling.

userStories.title: Add Google Drive integration — create folders and docs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create Google Drive folders and save generated briefs as Google Docs.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/google-drive.ts using googleapis SDK with service account auth
For quick requests: create Google Doc in configured 'Quick Requests' folder
For full projects: create folder structure — /Pearl Projects/[YYYY]/[Project Name]/ with subfolders: 01 - Brief, 02 - Assets (from client), 03 - Working Files, 04 - Deliverables, 05 - Feedback
Save brief as Google Doc in appropriate location (Quick Requests folder or 01 - Brief subfolder)
Return shareable links for folder and doc
Env vars: GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON, GOOGLE_PROJECTS_FOLDER_ID, GOOGLE_QUICK_REQUESTS_FOLDER_ID
Graceful error handling — if Drive fails, return error message but don't crash
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Service account auth — no user OAuth needed. UX spec constraint: errors must include fallback.

userStories.title: Add Monday.com integration — create tasks and projects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create Monday.com items from confirmed requests.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/monday.ts using Monday.com GraphQL API
For quick requests: create single item on configured quick requests board with columns for name, status, due date, requester, division, Drive folder link, brief doc link
For full projects: create group on full projects board with items for each deliverable, plus columns for status, due date, assignee, links
Return Monday.com item/board URL
Search function: searchItems(query) searches Monday boards by name and returns matching items with status and links
Env vars: MONDAY_API_TOKEN, MONDAY_QUICK_BOARD_ID, MONDAY_FULL_BOARD_ID
Graceful error handling — if Monday fails, return error but don't crash
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Monday GraphQL API. Column mappings will be configured via env or config file.

userStories.title: Wire up post-confirmation workflow execution
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, when I confirm my request, I want the bot to create the brief, folder, tasks, and notify the team.
acceptanceCriteria: When user confirms ('yes'), bot immediately sends 'Setting everything up...' message (UX spec: never leave user in silence)
Bot executes in sequence: generate brief → create Drive folder/doc → create Monday task/project → save project record to DB
On full success: bot sends completion message with all 3 links (brief, folder, Monday) matching UX spec format
Completion message includes 'The marketing team has been notified' and 'Reply to me anytime to check on status'
Completion message distinguishes infrastructure from work: says 'All set! Here's everything' not 'Everything is done' (UX spec constraint #10)
On partial failure: bot reports what succeeded and what failed, offers form as fallback (UX spec constraint #8)
Project record saved to projects table
Conversation status updated to 'complete'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Critical flow. UX spec constraints: never silent during processing, always show all 3 links, distinguish infrastructure from work.

userStories.title: Post notifications to #marketing-requests channel
userStories.description: As a marketing team member, I want to see new requests in #marketing-requests with summary and links.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful project creation, bot posts formatted notification to configured channel
Quick request format: ':inbox_tray: *Quick Request: [Title]*' with from, type, due, summary, links
Full project format: ':inbox_tray: *New Project: [Title]*' with from, type, due, summary, deliverables list, links
Notification includes 'React with :eyes: to claim this request'
Env var: SLACK_MARKETING_CHANNEL_ID
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec: notification IS the handoff — must contain everything needed to start work.

userStories.title: Implement status check workflow
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want to ask MarcomsBot about the status of my request.
acceptanceCriteria: When user sends status-related message, bot extracts project/request identifier
Bot searches Monday.com for matching item using searchItems()
Also searches local projects table for matching records
If found: returns formatted status with project name, status, assignee, due date, latest update, and links (matching UX spec status response format)
If not found: returns 'I couldn't find a project matching...' with suggestions (try different keywords, check #marketing-requests, ask in #marketing-team)
If multiple matches: returns list and asks user to pick
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec: status check is the #1 reason someone returns to the bot.

userStories.title: Implement search and retrieval workflow
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want to ask MarcomsBot to find a brief or project link.
acceptanceCriteria: When user sends search-related message ('find the brief for', 'link to', 'where's the'), bot extracts search terms
Searches local projects table and Monday.com
Returns matching project with brief doc link, folder link, and Monday link
If not found: suggests alternatives
If project predates bot: 'I can only find projects created through me. For older projects, check Monday directly'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec flow risk: user asks about project that predates bot.

userStories.title: Add conversation timeout and resume handling
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want the bot to remember my incomplete request if I walk away and come back.
acceptanceCriteria: Scheduled job checks for conversations where status='gathering' or 'confirming' AND updated_at > 24 hours ago
Bot sends timeout message to the original thread: 'Hey! We started a marketing request yesterday but didn't finish.' with what was collected and options to continue, start over, or cancel
If user replies 'continue', conversation resumes from where it left off
If user replies 'start over', conversation resets
If user replies 'cancel', conversation marked cancelled
If no response to timeout message after another 24 hours, conversation auto-cancelled silently
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec: timeout prompt after 24h. Don't auto-submit incomplete requests.

userStories.title: Add duplicate thread detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to handle users who message the bot from multiple threads simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: When a new conversation starts, check if user_id has an active (non-complete, non-cancelled) conversation
If active conversation exists in another thread: 'We have an open request in another thread — want to continue there or start fresh here?'
If user says 'continue there', bot links to the original thread
If user says 'start fresh', previous conversation is cancelled and new one begins
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec flow risk: user messages bot from multiple threads.

userStories.title: Add webhook endpoint for form submissions
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want to submit the intake form and have MarcomsBot automatically process it.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/webhook.ts exports Express or Bolt receiver route at /webhook/intake
Accepts POST with form fields: name, email, slack_username, division, request_type, description, deliverables, due_date, urgency, target_audience, key_messages, assets, references, notes
Maps form fields to same collected_data structure used by conversation flow
Classifies as quick or full using same classifyRequest() function
Executes same workflow: generate brief → Drive folder/doc → Monday task → notification
If slack_username provided, DMs user with confirmation and links
Project record saved with source='form'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Both paths (chat and form) use the same downstream workflow. Form just skips the conversation.

userStories.title: Add error handling and fallback messaging
userStories.description: As a Pearl staff member, I want clear error messages with alternatives when something goes wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: All external API calls (Claude, Google Drive, Monday) wrapped in try/catch
Error messages match UX spec format: describe what happened, say info is saved, offer form link as backup, offer #marketing-team as alternative
If Claude can't interpret a message: 'I didn't quite catch that. Could you rephrase? Or fill out the form instead: [link]'
If Drive fails during creation: continue with Monday, note Drive failure in completion message
If Monday fails during creation: continue with Drive, note Monday failure in completion message
Never show raw error messages or stack traces to users
Log all errors to console with context for debugging
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UX spec constraints: every error must include fallback, never leave user with zero output on partial failure.

userStories.title: Add configuration file and environment validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clean configuration system that validates all required credentials on startup.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/config.ts loads and validates all env vars on startup

Required vars: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_APP_TOKEN, SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET, SLACK_MARKETING_CHANNEL_ID, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON, GOOGLE_PROJECTS_FOLDER_ID, GOOGLE_QUICK_REQUESTS_FOLDER_ID, MONDAY_API_TOKEN, MONDAY_QUICK_BOARD_ID, MONDAY_FULL_BOARD_ID
Optional vars: INTAKE_FORM_URL (defaults to empty string)
If any required var missing, process exits with clear error listing which vars are missing
Export typed config object used by all modules
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Should be one of the first things loaded. Clear error on missing config prevents confusing runtime failures.

userStories.title: Add README with setup and deployment instructions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need documentation to set up and deploy MarcomsBot.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md covers: project overview, prerequisites, Slack app setup steps (permissions, event subscriptions, socket mode), Google service account setup, Monday.com API token setup, environment variables reference, local development instructions, deployment options (Railway, Render, Fly.io)
Includes architecture diagram showing: Slack events → Bot → Claude/Drive/Monday → Slack notifications
Lists all required Slack bot scopes: app_mentions:read, chat:write, im:read, im:write, channels:read, reactions:read
Lists all required Slack event subscriptions: app_mention, message.im
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Documentation for the team. Covers the Configuration Needed section of the PRD.
